Hey, what's going on everyone? This is probably the most important video that you will watch, but I probably say that a lot, but this is a really, really, really important video because at the end of the day, lead generation is the lifeblood of the business. If you do not have the first of the five core functions in, then you don't know what to do. You don't have new people walking in the door based on referrals, based on whatever, you need new blood in the business. That's how a business grows. If you don't have this dialed in, then the business won't succeed. This is the actual presentation that I gave at Jim Lawrence Summit 2018. For some reason, whatever reason, my videographer decided not to record the actual presentation. So I'm going to give you a treat and redo the presentation for you, which is cool because this is probably the most important video for you to understand exactly how we generate leads for our business and how we did it for the gyms and how we've gone into every market that we've ever gone into to make ads work. This is the exact process. The beauty of it is that it is methodical. It is not creative based. It's very straightforward and you don't need skill. You just need work ethic to make it work. So those are the kind of processes that I like. So let's get into it. One area, this is a quote from Jeff Bezos. One area where I think we are especially distinctive is failure. This is him describing Amazon. I believe we are the best place in the world to fail. We have plenty of practice. Failure and invention are inseparable twins. To invent, you have to experiment. If you know an event that it's going to work, it's not an experiment. Most large organizations embrace the idea of invention but are not willing to suffer the string of failed experiments necessary to get there. Outsize returns often come from betting against conventional wisdom. And conventional wisdom is usually right. Given a 10% chance of a hundred times payoff, you should take that bet every time. But you're still going to be wrong 9 times out of 10. We all know that if you swing for the fences, you're going to strike out a lot. But you're also going to hit some home runs. The difference between baseball and business, however, is that baseball has a truncated outcome distribution. When you swing, no matter how well you connect with the ball, the most runs you can get is more. In business, every once in a while, when you step up to the plate, you can score a thousand runs. This long tail distribution of returns is why it's important to be bold. Big winners pay for so many experiments. I think this is one of the most impactful paragraphs that I've ever read as a business owner. And it's one of the reasons that we are so big on innovation and trying things and trying things to improve the processes for ourselves and for you guys when we're making everything that's related to the gyms. I just want you to understand that same thing is nowhere more so than even lead generation, is that we're constantly evolving. The platforms evolve, the way that consumers respond evolve, headlines, copy hooks, whatever. All those things evolve over time and need to constantly be willing to pay a certain amount for failure. Obviously, we spend a lot of our own money to make sure that we can cut out a lot of that failure for you. But I just want you to understand, you just have to embrace that part of the process. Every time we did this lead gen 2.0 portal and we spent 40 grand at 16 locations, we probably saved three quarters of the ads that we made weren't winners. But once we had the winners, you can scale those. That's the game. So you just have to be prepared for that. So just to say, whenever someone's like, man, my leads aren't working or my ads aren't working, I'm like, okay, well, what have you done? Well, I remade the same four ads again, or I just turned them back on. That's not nearly the amount of effort that's required to really consistently dominate a marketplace. And you should expect more of yourself, honestly. You can do it. I actually literally told Mel, this is the process. Mel is not a marketer. Mel follows the videos and follow the process that I told her to break. I said, here's all the headlines we're going to test. Here's all the creative we're going to test. And here's all the copy that we're going to test. I want you to give me every version you can think of and test them in every market and then start finding the winners. That's all we did. So I just want to explain that to you before we get into it. So one main point here is that 80-20, just focusing on this one thing is going to be one of the main things. I can't tell you how many times I'll talk to Jim or just realize that when I realized that I looked at my revenue, it was directly proportional to how much I spent on marketing. That's probably something to take note of. If you have something that's creating a four to one return or five to one return or ten to one return, you should spend more on that thing, which is what marketing is. You have the capacity to fulfill it and work the leads, which most of you do. So most people just don't do it because of mental limitations they impose on themselves. There's no reason for it. Okay. Major in the main thing here is you want to make sure that these are the things that you're focusing on, not the tiny little like, split testing the button colors. You know what I mean? That stuff's not going to make a big difference. The big difference are going to be the headlines and the creative. Nine times out of ten, that's going to be the big things that move the needle. Do the boring work that makes you money. If you do this every single week and make five new ads, every single week that look different, feel different, et cetera, I guarantee you that you'll always be able to generate leads. All you have to do is actually do it. It's boring as hell. And then the Bruce Lee Thousand Kicks is basically, if you've heard the saying, he said, I don't fear a man who's done a thousand kicks one time, but one kick a thousand times. And so the more you follow this process, the better you will get at generating leads because the more confidence you'll have that you will always eventually crack it. At the end of the day, you need to take home this one belief, which is that no matter what you do, weight loss is not changing. That market is only growing and people want to lose weight. So all we have to do is get around their banner blindness and give them a promotion and give them creative that they will see and respond to. And just to give you some peace of mind, I can't tell you the amount of areas that we've been in, even where my old gyms were in California, in Southern California, where we work with gym launch gyms who start working with us. And I was running ads for six straight years in those areas. And my competitors were using the same ad, same copy as I was, right? And six years later, ads still work there. They don't look the same, but the offer still converts, which just shows you that people always want to lose weight. The average woman died five times a year. So you just need to adopt that as a belief that there are always people who want to sign up for the gym, which is have to get in front of them. So you have five, six different platforms that you generate leads from. Okay? So here are the principles for lead generation, especially in the most competitive markets. A lot of markets, you don't even need to do this, but in the most competitive markets, you need to create new ads weekly. One, two, once an offer is a winner, don't change it. So in this example, we already know what the winning offers are. Six-week challenges work. Whether you do a summer slim down, six-week challenge, a free six-week challenge, a free little black dress challenge, whatever it is, those are different wrapping paper on the same offer. All right? The offer is the hardest thing to figure out in a marketplace. But once you have the offer, it works. All we have to do is just wrap it up over and over again. That's what the different look and feel is, which is point number three. It just has to look and feel different. So if you're like, man, I made another video of me standing in front of the same wall pitching with a different t-shirt on. It says the same look and feel. You need a totally different look and feel for the ad for it to stick out again. All right? The fourth thing is you have to measure after 72 hours. All right? This is one of the hardest things to do. When I coach people, I coach them through this, but even when I run my own ads, I have to coach myself and it's really hard and I want to turn things off. But I can't say many times I've had winners that were losing in the beginning and then turned around. Day three, day four, day five, and then by day seven, they were actually my best ad. So sometimes early on numbers aren't perfect. All platforms have tweaks. You don't have issues with them. So, and imperfections. And the last thing is that there will always be eyeballs. There will always be people who are looking to lose weight. There will always be eyeballs that we can buy. And weight loss is honestly one of the easiest things to generate leads for because personally everyone wants it. And so as long as you have an acquisition process, like the one we do that is very profitable, then we can afford to buy it. And you also need to understand that, not that you don't already know this, but as long as we are profitable or breaking even in the acquisition, then we are making money. If I could tell you that tomorrow, pay $0 and get 20 EFTs at $200 a month, would you do it? Yes. But for some reason when I say spend two grand or spend four grand and make four grand, people are really hesitant to do it. Right? So you just have to think about it differently. Now, one of the big beliefs that I always have is that every single day, thousands of people in every marketplace sign up for a gym. And every single one of them responded to marketing. All of them. They heard it from a friend. That's a referral. They Googled online. It was either a Google ad that they clicked or it was off of SEO because of blog traffic that the person got them in the door. They went on Yop and looked for places with their views. So if you have a review strategy, that's something that's going to help you rank higher there. Or they responded to an ad off of Facebook or Instagram, whatever. The point is that no matter what, no one walks into a gym and signs up for membership without responding to marketing of some sort. And so the goal here is just to own as many of those traffic sources as possible. And that way we can dominate a marketplace. Now, negative headline would be like what big gyms won't tell you and then two negative faces. That could be on the in-shot decoration for the ad. So those are the types of things or curiosity like how this new program is sweeping the nation. Those are things like those are all different ways that we're adding tweaks to the headline. Copy, you've got short copy, medium copy, long copy. And then underneath of those are components of copy that work really well. So reason why a lot of people like if you're going to say something like they want to know why right? So we're doing a free six-week challenge. Why? Because we want to use your testimony to whatever. Why? Because we're up in our new facility. Why? Because we're up in a new program. Why? Like we have to give them a reason that makes sense. Right? And people like to know what the reason is. Because is one of the strongest words in persuasion. A lot of times if you just say because people will just trust you more. It's the weirdest thing in the world. They actually tested this in a funny study. People will just let you do things if you just say, hey, can I cut you in line? Because and then if you whatever follows because doesn't even need to make sense and they'll let you do it just because you put because in. It's weird. Whenever you're writing out benefits like a meal plan, you don't want to say a meal plan. You want to say meal plan that and then you want to try and tie things to status that will have you looking better than all your friends. Right? That'll be so easy that all your friends will be jealous of how you can eat. Right? And people will be better than like easy because it's increasing someone's status. It's a more powerful way of adding a benefit. So that you can write that's kind of like the that's where you translate what what this thing is for them. It's personalizing the individual pieces. Make it poetic. So you would describe the action, the feeling that's associated with it. So the jealousy thing. That's a feeling. So that you never have to think what am I going to cook for dinner tonight ever again. Those are feelings. So it's just like trying to say the same thing without saying it. It's just that does require a little bit of creativity. But if you just look at like, how can I say the same thing differently? Make it visual. So I'm giving like, you know, an example would be like so that you as you're working out like so steam's rising off your abs. Right? Like that's how that's how intense the workouts are. Whatever. So that's kind of a visual that people can see that makes sense to them. The next thing is implied authority. And what that's basically going to be is you want to imply authority as the person who's making the advertisement as writing the copy. Like we, you know, we have 1000 case studies. There's a reason that we're the place that everyone drives from over from two states over to come to those are things that will give you implied authority. Applied social proof. That's going to a lot of times be on the landing page. But if you can try and tie that in with testimonial or people who are working out in the facility, that goes a long way. The advanced part here is that where it's actually coming from. So if you're always advertising from your own page, one, two, three fitness, a lot of times it's good to buy or not buy. And so if you this is, these are the things that help with banner blindness, best Dallas deals, best Dallas fitness deals, Dallas fitness challenges. And obviously when I say local, you want to have it be hyper local. So you wouldn't want to say Dallas, you'd want to say, gosh, I don't even know. So for Austin, right? I'm in Austin, Texas. I wouldn't say Austin. I would say B cave because that's my like neighborhood area. That's my zip code. So you want to stick with the hyper local areas because then you're the response rate will be significantly better. Videos, all of these types of things work. The enhancers are things like use in shot to make it a meme, add background color, add contrast, add little doodads like smiley faces, exclamation points, little flames. You can make it black and white on video as well. You can do a professional versus iPhone. Those are two things to split test. We've worked with both really professional videos are really ugly looking videos. Both work. Always make sure to add subtitles because people 85% of the time aren't actually clicking to listen. Having flashcards to read on camera works really well. I've done that with pages where we actually say what's on the pages and flip through them. I've had many ads crush that for the six week challenge in the past. Scenery, you want to change up the background. Sometimes you want to do the gym, sometimes you want to do a blank wall, sometimes you want to have turf, sometimes you want to be outside the front door and the entrance to the gym. These are all different places that you can do the same video. If you're pitching towards the camera, these are all different ways that you can pitch differently. That's also one of the styles, personal pitching. That works. Let me show you around. It's a totally different style. You're welcoming them, you're walking with you, you're showing them the gym as you're explaining the promotion. This is where you'd start. You'd weigh in here. It's a little bit of a twist on the let me show you around. Group workout that's packed, those ones crush. If there's an easy one that right out the gate will almost always work, having a lot of people doing the same thing at the same time. Everyone doing bicycles, everyone doing jumping jacks, everyone doing push-ups on the same cadence, freaking converts like crazy. I don't know why it works the way it does. It just does. It's amazing. What would you say to someone who's never been here before? You have to have that kind of vibe. That is something that works really, really well. I just covered wall six push-ups. All that stuff works really well. The average Joe testimonial works well. It's just like Karen. She's like, hey, my name's Karen. I've been at it once or two fitness for three years and totally changed my life. They're really great here, blah, blah, blah. I started with the six-week challenge. It's a great way to get started. If you're interested in that, they're doing a free promotion. You should totally do it. You don't even have to have them mention it. You can say, listen to Karen's testimonial and she started with the six-week challenge. You should do that too. That would be in the copy. Different versions. Average Joe testimonial care cells where you have three or four videos of testimonials, so it just gives you even more social proof on the video. You want to make sure that you don't say how much weight they lost. You can say they dropped a few sizes and you don't want to put how much time. You're going to be like, I lost 20 pounds in six weeks. You don't want to say that because you'll just get your out of count band. You might get away with this sometimes, but most times you'll just get banned and it's a pain in the ass. Don't do that. You want to say, since I've been with Joe, I have lost 30 pounds. That's a little bit more gray area. It's a little bit more doable. Since I've been with Joe, I've lost three dress sizes and my blood pressure has improved tremendously and I can pick up my kids. That kind of stuff. The other is obviously just a person in the video doing this, capturing the testimonial. You can make it captured professionally or you can capture it with an iPhone or you can do it with a selfie. All three of those are different versions that are enhancers that give a different look and feel. New, these are boomerang and gift style where you just like, these are like eight second, 10 second videos where you're like having someone jump on a box and back down, jump on the box and back down. Or if you're like waving a banner that says like six weeks free and it just repeats over and over again or you do like a butt twerk and then it comes up and it's all in 10 seconds, those tend to work really well right now. Those are all things, all variations that have worked really well. Actions for you to keep up on this is just follow me, follow Grant Cardo, Gary V, Tyler Lopez, Ladyboss, Thomas Delafler, all guys who run in serious amounts of traffic. Look at the styles of the videos that they're making. Those are going to be typically the things that are going to work best. All right. So again, these are all the variables.
